home.hero_title: Travel through the past, protect the future.
home.hero_subtitle: Authentic journeys along the Silk Road
home.intro_title: At the heart of Central Asia, Uzbekistan reveals its treasures between mythical cities and preserved villages.
home.intro_body: A land of legends on the Silk Road, it offers the majestic beauty of Samarkand and Bukhara, the warmth of Uzbek hospitality, and landscapes ranging from oases to deserts and mountains. A country of culture, encounters and living traditions, Uzbekistan captivates with its authenticity, historical richness and the generosity of its people.
home.founder_title: People at the heart of every encounter.
home.founder_body1: My name is Navbakhor Boudot and I created AFOR to build bridges between my two cultures – France and Uzbekistan – and offer meaningful tourism: solidarity-based, authentic and deeply human.
home.founder_body2: Our trips are not simply tours, but human experiences that give meaning, enrich and bring people together. You will not only be a spectator — you will listen to the stories of those who keep traditions alive.

footer.tagline: The bridge between Uzbekistan and France
footer.address_note: In-person consultations in Lyon by appointment

circuits.section_subtitle: Unique experiences at the heart of the Silk Road
circuits.section_conversion_note: Every itinerary can be tailored to your pace, dates, and travel style. We design your journey with you, not for you.
circuits.c1_desc: From Millennial Cities to Holy Monuments: Bukhara, Samarkand. A perfect introduction to the history of the Silk Road, punctuated by moments of silence and admiration.
circuits.c2_desc: Between millennial cities and discovery of Uzbek countryside traditions. Dive into local life and share family daily life, in a spirit of fair tourism.
circuits.c3_desc: From vibrant cities to authentic countryside: a deep discovery of Uzbekistan. A slower pace to absorb the soul of the country.
circuits.c4_desc: Comfort & Authenticity Itinerary. An extended and deep dive into the wonders of Uzbekistan, combining cultural discovery and unique human encounters.
circuits.page_hero_subtitle: Four itineraries crafted for the soul of the Silk Road
circuits.page_intro: Each tour is an invitation to travel differently — with intention, curiosity and respect. Far from the crowds, close to the people, you will cross thousand-year-old landscapes and live encounters that leave a lasting mark.
circuits.page_tailor_body: Each itinerary is a starting point. We adapt it to your pace, your dates, and your travel style. Contact us to co-design your ideal trip.
circuits.page_cta_title: Ready to travel the Silk Road?
circuits.page_cta_body: Our advisors guide you every step of the way to create a unique, solidarity-based, and unforgettable journey.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_subtitle: From millennial cities to sacred monuments: a perfect introduction to the Silk Road, punctuated by moments of silence and wonder.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_intro: To explore Samarkand, Bukhara and Shakhrisabz, we recommend a minimum of 7 days. In fewer than 7 days, it would be difficult to discover the full beauty of these cities.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_intro2: During this journey, you will discover the most beautiful cities of Uzbekistan at your own pace, in small groups, accompanied by your local guide throughout the trip.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_intro3: Your programme can be adjusted, moved forward or modified to suit your preferences:
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_intro_bullet1: Night train between Samarkand and Bukhara possible
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_intro_bullet2: Option for a free day in Samarkand
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_day1_body: Arrival in Samarkand, welcome and check-in at the hotel. First immersion with a visit to the Registan, the most emblematic monumental complex in Central Asia. Dinner in the city.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_day2_body: A full day exploring the city's treasures: Gur-e-Amir mausoleum (Tamerlane's tomb), Bibi-Khanym mosque and the lively Siab bazaar. In the afternoon, visit Shakhrisabz, Tamerlane's birthplace, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_day3_body: Transfer to Bukhara (by train or car based on preference). First discovery: the Chor-Minor necropolis and silk artisan workshops. Free evening to wander the alleys of the historic centre.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_day4_body: A full day dedicated to Bukhara's historic centre (UNESCO listed): the Ark citadel, the Kalon mosque and its minaret, the Samanid mausoleum, and the Toq-i-Sarrafon bazaar. Encounters with local artisans — potters, weavers, embroiderers.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_day5_body: Excursion to the surroundings: Chor-Bakr complex (necropolis of the Prophet's brothers), Sitorai Mokhi-Khosa summer palace, and a glimpse of the desert and silk worm farms in season.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_day6_body: Return journey to Samarkand. Free time to revisit favourite sites, shopping at the market and lunch with a local family. Farewell dinner with your guide.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_day7_body: Transfer to the airport according to your flight time. Journey's end, heart full of Silk Road memories.
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_include2: Accommodation in hotel or riad (6 nights)
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_include3: Private French-speaking guide throughout the stay
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_include5: Entrance fees to monuments in the programme
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_practical_visa: No visa required for French citizens
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_practical_flight: Direct flight from Paris (~6h) or Lyon via Istanbul
circuits.samarkand_boukhara_cta_body: This tour is a starting point. We tailor it to your dates, pace, and travel style. Write to us to co-design your ideal journey.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_title: A Solidarity Journey through the Heart of Uzbekistan
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_subtitle: Between ancient cities and Uzbek countryside, a deep dive into local life, living traditions and the warmth of Uzbek hospitality.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_intro: This 11-day journey takes you through Karakalpakstan, Khiva, Bukhara and Samarkand to Tashkent, combining UNESCO World Heritage sites with deep immersion in rural Uzbek life and local families.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_intro2: You will explore regions rarely visited by mass tourism: the unique museums of the Aral desert, silk and woodcraft artisans in Khiva, and farming families of the Khorezm region.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_intro3: Your programme can be tailored to your preferences:
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_intro_bullet1: Homestay night in the Khorezm region available
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_intro_bullet2: Visit to AFOR reforestation project in Gurlan included
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_intro_bullet3: Option: free day in Samarkand or Tashkent
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day1_body: Arrival in Tashkent, welcome and hotel check-in. First walk through the historic Khast Imam quarter and the Chorsu bazaar. Welcome dinner.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day2_title: Day 2 — Flight to Nukus & Savitsky Museum
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day2_body: Domestic flight to Urgench then transfer to Nukus, capital of Karakalpakstan. Visit the celebrated Savitsky Museum, one of the world's greatest avant-garde art collections, secretly assembled during the Soviet era.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day3_title: Day 3 — Desert Fortresses & the Aral Sea
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day3_body: Excursion into the Karakalpakstan steppes: ancient fortresses of Chilpik Kala and Ayaz Kala, relics of the Khorezmian civilisation. A moving visit to the abandoned port of Moynaq on the former Aral Sea.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day4_title: Day 4 — Khiva, a Living Open-Air Museum
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day4_body: Road to Khiva. Exploration of Ichan Kala (UNESCO-listed old town): Kalta Minor minaret, Tash-Hauli palace, Juma mosque. Meeting local artisans specialising in wood carving and embroidery. Visit to Gurlan and the AFOR reforestation project.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day4_highlight: Ichan Kala, Kalta Minor, AFOR project
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day5_title: Day 5 — Road to Bukhara & Silk Weaving
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day5_body: Drive to Bukhara through the Uzbek countryside. Stop at a silk weaving village to discover the ancestral ikat technique. Homestay night possible depending on dates.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day6_body: Full day in Bukhara: the Ark citadel, Kalon mosque and its 47-metre minaret, Samanid mausoleum (9th century), and the historic Toq covered bazaars. Free evening in the medina's alleyways.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day7_body: Discovery of Bukhara's madrasas: Mir-i-Arab, Nadir Divanbegi. Visit to Lyabi-Hauz, heart of the historic quarter. Lunch with a local family and afternoon with potter or embroiderer artisans.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day8_body: Road to Samarkand. First immersion with a visit to the Registan at dusk. Dinner in a traditional city restaurant.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day9_body: Gur-e-Amir mausoleum, Bibi-Khanym mosque, Shah-i-Zinda necropolis. Afternoon: excursion to Shakhrisabz, Tamerlane's birthplace, to visit the Ak-Saray palace and the Dor-us-Saodat complex.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day10_body: Road or fast train to Tashkent. Visit to the old town: Kukeldash mosque and Chorsu bazaar. Free time for last-minute shopping. Farewell dinner with your guide.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_day11_body: Transfer to the airport according to your flight time. End of your solidarity journey, heart full of unforgettable human encounters.
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_include2: Accommodation in hotel or guesthouse (10 nights)
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_include3: 1 homestay night in the Uzbek countryside
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_include4: Private French-speaking guide throughout the stay
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_include6: Entrance fees to monuments in the programme
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_include7: Contribution to the AFOR reforestation project in Gurlan
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_practical_visa: No visa required for French citizens
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_practical_flight: Direct flight from Paris (~6h) or Lyon via Istanbul
circuits.voyage_solidaire_11j_cta_body: This tour is a starting point. We tailor it to your dates, pace, and travel style. Write to us to co-design your ideal solidarity journey.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_title: Two weeks of immersion at the heart of Uzbekistan
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_subtitle: From ancient cities to preserved villages: a deep discovery of Uzbekistan, at a slow pace to absorb its soul.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_intro: This 14-day journey takes you through Karakalpakstan, Khiva, Bukhara, Samarkand, the Fergana Valley and Tashkent, combining UNESCO World Heritage sites with deep immersion in rural Uzbek life and local families.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_intro2: Accompanied by a passionate French-speaking guide, you will discover Uzbekistan's millennial gems, as well as the country's nature and countryside. Meetings will be arranged with locals to discover local traditions and immerse yourself in Uzbek culture.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_intro3: Your programme is of course fully customizable:
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_intro_bullet1: Homestay nights in the Khorezm region available
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_intro_bullet2: Learning traditional arts (pottery, weaving, music, cooking) possible
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_intro_bullet3: Free day in Samarkand or Tashkent as an option
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_programme_title: Suggested itinerary for two weeks in Uzbekistan
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day1_body: Flight from Lyon or Paris to Urgench (via Istanbul or Tashkent). Arrival at Urgench airport, then transfer to Khiva. Visit to the museum city located in the heart of an oasis, built with rammed earth and mud bricks. Overnight at the hotel.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day2_body: Continue visiting Khiva in the morning. Lunch, then visit to the brand-new Nurillabay complex: Khorezm history museum and art exhibition. Departure for Nukus. Overnight in a guesthouse.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day3_title: Day 3 — The Louvre of the Desert & Fortresses
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day3_body: Visit to the Savitsky Museum, nicknamed the 'Louvre of the Desert', housing the incredible collection of over 80,000 paintings saved by Igor Savitsky from Stalinist censorship. Overnight with a camel-herder family in Kungrad.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day4_body: Visit to the desert fortresses. Encounters with farmers and an Uzbek family. Preparation of the national dish plov with them, a stroll along the Amu Darya, visit to a lake and surrounding farmland. Overnight with a local family.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day5_body: Early morning departure to admire a sunrise in the Uzbek desert. Stops and visits along the route (460 km). Arrival in Bukhara in the evening. Overnight in a family hotel.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day6_body: Visit to the old city of Bukhara: its mausoleums and the Ark Citadel. Cultural and historical discoveries. Walking tours and exploration of the city. Overnight in a family hotel.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day7_body: Morning excursion from Bukhara to discover the surrounding countryside. Visits to sacred sites (Naqshbandi), the last emir's summer residence (Sitorai Mokhi-Khosa), and the unique Chor-Minor madrasa. Overnight in a family hotel.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day8_body: Departure from Bukhara to Sentob (3h, 270 km), a charming Tajik village. Nestled in a true haven of greenery amid orchards, this is the ideal place to wander and rest. Meet your host family, share a meal and spend the night with them.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day9_body: Hiking according to your wishes: from a short stroll to a several-hour trek, you choose the route that suits you to discover the surroundings (fortress, archaeological site, flora and fauna). Possible excursion to a waterfall and its natural pools. Overnight in a guesthouse.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day10_body: Departure to Samarkand (220 km, 3h). Discover the capital of the Timurid Empire: Ulugh Beg Observatory, bazaar, madrasas. Sound-and-light show in the evening on the great Registan square. Overnight at hotel or guesthouse.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day11_body: Visit to historic buildings: Gur-e-Amir mausoleum (Tamerlane's tomb), Bibi-Khanym mosque, Shah-i-Zinda necropolis. Silk weaving workshop. Free or guided strolls in Samarkand. Overnight at the hotel.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day12_body: Road to the Fergana Valley, the breadbasket of Uzbekistan. Discover Margilan and its famous Atlas silk workshops. Meetings with silk weaver artisans. Overnight in Fergana.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day13_body: Train to Tashkent (3h) in the morning. Visit to the modern city then the old city: mausoleums, mosques and squares. Free or guided strolls, free time. Overnight at the hotel.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_day14_body: Discovery of the great Chorsu bazaar, one of the most beautiful in Central Asia. Last purchases and souvenirs. Transfer to the airport for the return flight.
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_include2: Accommodation in hotel or guesthouse (13 nights)
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_include3: Homestay nights in the Khorezm region
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_include4: Private French-speaking guide throughout the stay
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_include6: Entrance fees to monuments in the programme
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_include8: Contribution to the AFOR reforestation project in Gurlan
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_practical_visa: No visa required for French citizens
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_practical_flight: Direct flight from Paris (~6h) or Lyon via Istanbul
circuits.immersion_totale_14j_cta_body: This tour is a starting point. We tailor it to your dates, pace, and travel style. Write to us to co-design your ideal journey.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_title: Grand Tour of Uzbekistan — Comfort & Authenticity
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_subtitle: From Tashkent to the Aral Desert, from Khiva to the Fergana Valley: an extended and deep dive into the wonders of Uzbekistan.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_intro: This 18-day grand tour takes you through the full diversity of Uzbekistan: Tashkent, Karakalpakstan, Nukus, Khiva, Bukhara, Samarkand, the Fergana Valley and Andijan. A comprehensive exploration of the country, combining UNESCO World Heritage sites, authentic countryside and unique human encounters.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_intro2: Accompanied by a passionate French-speaking guide, you will discover the thousand-year-old jewels of the Silk Road, as well as the nature, artisans and local families of regions rarely frequented by mass tourism.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_intro3: Your programme is fully customisable to your wishes:
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_intro_bullet1: Homestay nights in the Khorezm region available
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_intro_bullet2: Visit to AFOR reforestation project in Gurlan included
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_intro_bullet3: Traditional craft workshops (pottery, weaving, cooking) optional
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day1_body: Arrival in Tashkent, welcome and hotel transfer. First stroll through the historic Khast Imam district, the Kaffal Shashi Mosque and the famous Chorsu Bazaar. Welcome dinner at a traditional restaurant.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day2_body: A day to discover the modern capital: the Museum of the History of Uzbekistan, the Amir Timur complex, the Tashkent TV Tower, and the wide avenues inherited from Soviet architecture. Free evening to explore trendy restaurants and cafés.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day2_highlight: History Museum, Tashkent Tower, Amir Timur
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day3_title: Day 3 — Flight to Nukus & Savitsky Museum
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day3_body: Domestic flight to Urgench then transfer to Nukus, capital of Karakalpakstan. Visit the famous Savitsky Museum — nicknamed the 'Louvre of the Desert' — housing over 80,000 works saved from Soviet censorship, including an extraordinary avant-garde collection.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day3_highlight: Savitsky Museum — Louvre of the Desert
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day4_body: Excursion into the steppes of Karakalpakstan: ancient fortresses of Chilpik Kala and Ayaz Kala, remnants of the Khorezm civilisation. A moving visit to the abandoned port of Moynaq on the former Aral Sea, a stark symbol of ecological catastrophe.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day5_body: Drive to Khiva. Exploration of Ichan Kala (UNESCO-listed old city): Kalta Minor minaret, Tash-Hauli palace, Juma Mosque with its 213 carved wooden columns. Meeting with local artisans specialising in wood carving and traditional embroidery.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day6_body: Morning to deepen the discovery of Khiva and its hidden treasures. Lunch, then a visit to the village of Gurlan and the AFOR reforestation project: meeting with artisans and beneficiaries of the tree-planting programme to combat desertification.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day6_highlight: Khiva — Islam Khoja palace, AFOR Gurlan project
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day7_title: Day 7 — Road to Bukhara & Silk Villages
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day7_body: Drive to Bukhara through the Uzbek countryside (460 km). Stops in weaving villages to discover the ancestral techniques of ikat — silk with geometric patterns. Arrival in Bukhara in the evening, dinner in the medina.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day8_body: A day in Bukhara: the Ark Citadel (4th century), Kalon Mosque with its 47m minaret, the Samanid Mausoleum (9th century — the oldest brick monument in Central Asia), and the Toqs trading quarter. Free evening in the lanes of the UNESCO-listed historic medina.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day9_body: Discovery of Bukhara's madrasas: Mir-i-Arab (16th century), Nadir Divanbegi with its stunning decor. Visit of Lyabi-Hauz, the heart of the historic district. Lunch with a local family and afternoon with potter or embroidery artisans in their workshops.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day10_body: An excursion outside Bukhara to discover the surrounding countryside: the Chor-Bakr funerary complex (necropolis of the Prophet's brothers), the summer residence of the last emir Sitorai Mokhi-Khosa, and the unique four-minaret madrasa Chor-Minor. Return in the evening for dinner.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day11_body: Drive to Samarkand. Afternoon arrival and first immersion with a visit to the Registan at twilight — the most iconic monumental complex in Central Asia. Sound and light show possible in the evening. Dinner at a traditional restaurant.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day12_body: Visit the flagship monuments: Gur-e-Amir mausoleum (Tamerlane's tomb, 14th century), Bibi-Khanym Mosque (the largest mosque built in its era). Discover Ulugh Beg's observatory and its collection of astronomical globes. Silk weaving workshop.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day13_body: Morning at the Shah-i-Zinda necropolis, the avenue of mausoleums of Tamerlane's family with dazzling turquoise ceramics. Afternoon: excursion to Shakhrisabz, Tamerlane's birthplace (UNESCO-listed), to visit the grandiose Ak Saray palace and the Dor-us-Saodat complex.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day14_body: A day at your own pace: revisit your favourite places, stroll through the Siab Bazaar (Samarkand's oldest covered market), artisan shopping or hiking in the surrounding hills. Farewell dinner in Samarkand with your guide.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day15_body: Drive to the Fergana Valley, Uzbekistan's agricultural heartland and cradle of silk. Discovery of Margilan and its famous Atlas silk workshops: visit to the Yodgorlik factory, one of the last to produce natural silk dyed with indigo using traditional methods.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day16_body: Morning in Fergana to explore the local museum and Quvasoy market. Afternoon: visit to Kokand, the 19th-century capital of the Khanate of Kokand, to admire the Khan's palace (Khodayar Khan) and the Dakhma-i-Shakhon mosque.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day17_body: Morning in Andijan, birthplace of Babur, founder of the Mughal Empire. Visit the bazaar and Jami mosque. Fast train or flight to Tashkent. Closing evening with a farewell dinner at a gastronomic Uzbek restaurant.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day17_highlight: Andijan, bazaar, closing dinner in Tashkent
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_day18_body: Transfer to the airport according to your flight time. End of your grand tour, heart enriched by 18 days of discovery, encounters and wonder on the Silk Road.
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_include2: Accommodation in comfort hotel or guesthouse (17 nights)
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_include3: Optional homestay nights in the Khorezm region
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_include4: Private French-speaking guide throughout the stay
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_include6: Entrance fees to monuments in the programme
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_include7: 1 lunch and 1 dinner with a local family
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_include8: Visit and contribution to the AFOR project in Gurlan
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_include9: Craft workshop of your choice (silk, pottery, embroidery)
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_practical_visa: No visa required for French citizens
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_practical_flight: Direct flight from Paris (~6h) or Lyon via Istanbul
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_cta_title: Experience the Full Breadth of Uzbekistan
circuits.grand_circuit_18j_cta_body: This grand tour is a starting point. We tailor it to your dates, pace, and travel style. Write to us to co-design your ideal journey.

commitments.section_subtitle: Each departure supports concrete actions for local communities and future generations.
commitments.section_footer_note: By traveling with AFOR, you support a local chain of partners, artisans, host families, and education initiatives.
commitments.c1_desc: Fighting desertification and improving the quality of life of residents in Gurlan, Khorezm region.
commitments.c2_desc: Funding university studies for young people from disadvantaged backgrounds to make them tomorrow's ambassadors.
commitments.c3_desc: Supporting and educating young girls to support their independence and crucial role in society.

projects.hero_subtitle: Concrete actions for a responsible future
projects.intro: The France–Uzbekistan Roots Association works for environmental preservation and support for sustainable development in Uzbekistan. Its action is based on three main pillars: tree planting, support for youth education, and promotion of female entrepreneurship.
projects.intro2: Every year, concrete initiatives are implemented to contribute to a more responsible and solidarity-based future.
projects.p1_body: One of the association's major projects is tree planting to fight desertification and improve the quality of life of residents. In 2024, 30 trees were planted in Gurlan, a village located 58 km from Khiva, in the direction of Nukus. The goal is to green this region by involving tourists and the local community. In 2025, several young girls are being supported in their education.
projects.p2_body: The association funds university studies for young people from disadvantaged backgrounds, enabling them to become local ambassadors of change and carry forward the general interest. In 2024, 2 students were supported through this program, giving these young people the chance to access quality education and actively participate in their country's future.
projects.p3_body: AFOR also supports female entrepreneurship in Uzbekistan, particularly by assisting women with their business projects. In 2024, 3 women benefited from this support, enabling them to strengthen their skills and launch sustainable economic initiatives.
projects.cta_body: Every journey with AFOR directly contributes to these projects. Contact us to learn more.
